All communion with the nations forbidden, HEN the Lord. thy God shall bring thee into the land whither thou goest to possess it, and hath cast out many nations before thee, the Hittites, and the Girgashites, and the Amorites, and the Canaanitesg, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and. the Jebusites, seven nations greater and mightier than thou;

CHAPTER VII. All communion with the nations forbidden, HEN the Lord. thy God shall bring thee into the land whither thou goest to possess it, and hath cast out many nations before thee, the Hittites, and the Girgashites, and the Amorites, and the Canaanitesg, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and. the Jebusites, seven nations greater and mightier than thou;
The seven nations referred to are the Canaanites, Hittites, Hivites, Perizzites, Girgashites, Amorites, and the Jebusites (Joshua 3:10).

Go thou unto Phison, and take from thence a precious stone, and fix it in the ark to illuminate you: with the measure of a cubit (or span) shalt thou complete it above. And a door shalt thou set in the side of the ark; and with dwelling-places, inferior, second, and third, shalt thou make it.

my chosen. Israel is the chosen people, as the Lord said through Moses: "The Lord thy God hath chosen thee to be a special people unto himself, above all people that [are] upon the face of the earth" (Deut. 7:6). Many are called to come unto Christ and receive his power and blessings, but few are chosen to receive all he offers (D&C 95:5; 121:34). Those who are chosen are the faithful (3 Ne.…

Jose, the Galilian, in the way of homeletics, make thy ear like the hopper [to receive his words]." Redemption By Elder LeGrand R. Curtis Jr. Of the Seventy Through Christ, people can and do change their lives and obtain redemption. There are various names by which reference is made to the Lord Jesus Christ. These names give us insight into different aspects of the Lord’s atoning mission. Take, for example, the title “Savior.” We all have a sense of what it means to be saved because each of us…

There are various names by which reference is made to the Lord Jesus Christ. These names give us insight into different aspects of the Lord’s atoning mission. Take, for example, the title “Savior.” We all have a sense of what it means to be saved because each of us…
